prompt: Analyze test coverage gaps across a TypeScript project and generate missing tests. The project source is at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/project/src/' and existing tests are at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/project/tests/'. There are 25 source files (100-400 lines each) and 15 test files (50-200 lines each). Your task: 1) Read every source file and every test file (40 files total). 2) For each source file, catalog: all exported functions and methods, all exported classes with their public methods, all exported types/interfaces, all error handling paths (try/catch blocks), all conditional branches. 3) For each test file, catalog: which source file it tests, which functions/methods are tested, which edge cases are covered, which error paths are tested. 4) Compute coverage gaps: source files with no corresponding test file, exported functions with no test coverage, error handling paths not tested, boundary conditions not tested (null, undefined, empty string, zero, max values), conditional branches with only one path tested. 5) For the top 5 most critical uncovered source files, generate test files at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/project/tests/' following the existing test patterns: same test framework and assertion style, same file naming convention, same describe/it structure. Each generated test should cover: happy path for each function, error cases, edge cases, and boundary conditions. 6) Create a coverage analysis report at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/test-coverage-analysis.txt' with: coverage matrix (source file x test status), uncovered functions list, critical gaps (untested error handling, untested edge cases in core modules), generated test summary (what was created, what still needs manual review), and a prioritized list of test files to write next. CRITICAL: This is for an FDA-regulated medical device. You must also: 1) Analyze MC/DC coverage for safety-critical paths. 2) Generate mutation testing reports. 3) Create risk-based test prioritization. 4) Validate traceability to requirements. 5) Generate audit-ready documentation.
